title: Mélenchon holds first campaign rally in Saint-Denis as part of identity-based 'new France' strategy
subhead: The election of Bally Bagayoko and Aly Diouara as mayors in the Paris-region department of Seine-Saint-Denis in March were symbolically significant for Jean-Luc Mélenchon. Since 2018, the radical-left leader has made the area a testing ground for his 'new France' concept, as he is counting on the vote of its working-class citizens from immigrant communities for next year's presidential election.

title: 'No one was prepared for what was about to happen': Gaza flotilla activist speaks about violence she suffered in Israel
Armenians vote in pivotal legislative elections for their country's future, regional peace
Mirra Andreeva defies all odds to win first Grand Slam at French Open
subhead: The 19-year-old Russian prevailed on Saturday in the final (6-3, 6-2) against Poland's surprise contender Maja Chwalinska. The result capped two weeks in which the world number 8 handled the pressure, unlike the other tournament favorites.

title: French telecom giants reach deal to acquire struggling rival SFR
subhead: The companies – Bouygues Telecom, Orange and Free owner Iliad – said SFR parent Altice had accepted their offer of €20.35 billion for the struggling mobile operator. The deal could reshape the French telecoms landscape by reducing the number of main operators from four to three.

title: Women's desire, a men's issue?
subhead: No, women should not feel pressured to boost their libidos when they wane, writes sex columnist Maïa Mazaurette, who urges both men and women to reconsider female desire from a societal perspective.

title: France revives Gratitude Train for the US's 250th anniversary
subhead: A restored railcar from northern France will retrace the journey of the Gratitude Train, which carried gifts from Le Havre to New York in 1949.

Bernadette Chirac, the wife of ex-president Jacques Chirac, has died
subhead: An unfailing supporter of her husband's career, Bernadette Chodron de Courcel became a key figure a French politics. She was elected local councilor, a rare feat for a 'wife of,' and was very involved in charity work. She died at the age of 93.

title: Over three decades, China's promise became a trap for European industry
subhead: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (1/4). In just a few decades, China has gone from being the world's factory to a major technological rival for Europeans, who had hoped to profit on China's vast domestic market but underestimated its capacity for innovation.
byline: Bastien Bonnefous,  Isabelle Chaperon and  Sophie Fay

title: Zelensky's letter to Putin is actually addressed to Russia's elites who are tired of the war in Ukraine
subhead: Behind the offer of an outstretched hand, the Ukrainian president's letter to the Russian leader seeks to exploit Moscow's vulnerabilities and the Russian people's exhaustion after four years of high-intensity war.
byline: Benjamin Quénelle,  Claire Gatinois and  Thomas d’Istria
